summary: Gruber follows up on his earlier post about Steve Jobs calling his writing 'very insightful and not negative' 15 years ago, correcting his claim that he never acknowledged it on Daring Fireball. Long-time readers reminded him that he did acknowledge it — in the most Gruber way possible — by making 'Insightful and Not Negative' the slogan under the site's logo banner. He shares the original 2010 logo graphic and reflects on its 1× resolution predating retina displays. The post traces the history of DF's rotating logo slogans, from 'Mac Punditry and Curmudgeonry' to playful quips like 'Now With Retsyn.' He's now restored 'Very Insightful and Not Negative' as the current slogan and plans to bring back the tradition of occasional whimsical slogans.
key_points: Gruber discovered he did acknowledge the Steve Jobs quote, but only as an Easter egg in the site's logo slogan — not in any searchable post text
The 2010 logo graphic is a time capsule of the pre-retina era, rendered in Susan Kare's pixel font and now appearing blurry on modern displays
Daring Fireball had a long tradition of rotating slogans under the logo, from descriptive to playful, that Gruber had largely abandoned in recent years
Gruber is reviving the slogan tradition, putting 'Very Insightful and Not Negative' back and planning more whimsical entries
money_quotes: I did acknowledge it, in what is clearly the most Daring-Fireball-ish way possible: I made it the slogan under the logo banner.
That it was only ever a slogan in a PNG logo graphic explains why I couldn't find it by searching.
I can't remember if I omitted the very before insightful for aesthetic length, humility, or because I somehow thought it was punchier. But my 2025 self thinks it was a mistake to omit it.
In April 2010, 2× retina resolution wasn't yet a thing.
Feels like the right time to bring back a little whimsy, so I've put 'Very Insightful and Not Negative' back as the slogan for now.
I decided to just sit there with a smug look on my face for a few days (which, arguably, isn't all that different from what I do most days) and pretend that it was no big deal.
key_insight: Gruber's understated acknowledgment of the Steve Jobs quote — hiding it in a logo slogan rather than writing about it — perfectly captures both his editorial sensibility and Daring Fireball's voice.
